metadata.title: Zohal — AI acquisition workflow for Saudi real estate investors
metadata.description: Give Zohal your buy box and get serious property candidates with evidence, scenarios, renovation exposure, and clear next steps.

brand.name: Zohal
brand.tagline: Your AI-powered real estate acquisition team.

hero.headline: Your AI-powered real estate acquisition team
hero.subhead: Give Zohal your buy box. It sources serious Saudi property opportunities, verifies the evidence, models scenarios, estimates renovation exposure, and gives you a live workspace to decide what to do next.
hero.proofLine: Mandate-first sourcing • Evidence-linked deal files • Renovation-aware scenarios

credibilityStrip.label: Built for serious Saudi investors
items: Launch focus: active Riyadh investors buying SAR 1.5M-5M residential or mixed-use opportunities
Buy-box interpretation, candidate sourcing, comparable context, and diligence tracking
Renovation scope ranges, capex bands, confidence levels, and return impact
Evidence labels separate verified sources, market signals, modeled outputs, counterparty claims, and open diligence

problem.title: Investors start with a mandate, not a listing.
body: A serious buyer already knows the budget, neighborhoods, asset type, financing limits, return target, renovation appetite, timeline, and risk tolerance. The hard part is turning that intent into a researched shortlist before the opportunity disappears.

Zohal works like a high-output acquisition analyst: it turns the buy box into candidates, normalizes messy property information, builds comparable and valuation context, flags diligence gaps, coordinates missing information, and preserves the decision record.

sideCard.title: From scattered search to acquisition workflow
before: Listings, brokers, private groups, classifieds, and WhatsApp threads stay fragmented
Comparable context, valuation logic, and renovation exposure arrive too late
Broker follow-up, missing documents, and partner comments disappear across chats
after: The mandate becomes a ranked candidate pipeline with clear fit reasons
Each serious property gets a live workspace with evidence, scenarios, capex logic, and next steps
Decisions, assumptions, diligence items, and counterparty replies stay in one shared memory

howItWorks.title: From buy box to decision-ready workspace.
howItWorks.subhead: Zohal follows the same core loop every time: mandate intake, opportunity sourcing, research and enrichment, evaluation, renovation logic, and diligence coordination.

steps.title: Give Zohal the mandate
steps.body: Share the buy box: budget, neighborhoods, asset type, financing constraints, target returns, renovation appetite, timeline, and risk tolerance.

steps.title: Receive serious candidates
steps.body: Zohal sources and ranks opportunities, enriches property facts, builds comparable context, and marks what is verified, modeled, claimed, or still uncertain.

steps.title: Model, coordinate, and decide
steps.body: Each serious candidate becomes a live acquisition workspace with scenarios, renovation ranges, diligence gaps, broker follow-up, and a clear next action.

items.label: High-stakes steps can wait for checked fields and human sign-off
Translation and localization review in one workflow
For every extracted field and flagged issue
Teams focus on mismatches instead of re-checking every page

stats.footnote: Final judgment stays with your team. Zohal helps you move faster without hiding the source, the checks, or the review decisions behind each result.

capabilities.title: What Zohal checks that generic AI does not.

tabs.label: Click-to-source fields
tabs.title: Important facts come out in a form your team can verify field by field.
bullets: Key details like parties, dates, totals, obligations, and terms are proposed as structured claims
Each claim stays linked to source evidence
Unclear or unsupported claims stay marked for review instead of being guessed

tabs.label: Generator + verifier
tabs.title: A second verifier checks unsupported, conflicting, or risky output before it moves.
bullets: A first pass proposes claims and a second pass challenges them
Missing support, contradictions, and policy conflicts are surfaced before anything is sent
Reviewers focus on exceptions instead of re-checking every page

tabs.label: Arabic + English workflows
tabs.title: Handle bilingual documents, translation checks, and localization review in one review flow.
bullets: Bring in files from uploads, WhatsApp, Google Drive, and OneDrive
Arabic and English documents can be reviewed together with translation and localization mismatches surfaced early
On supported web workflows, Zohal can process extracted document data without storing the original file

tabs.label: Decision packs
tabs.title: The workflow ends in something operational, not a chat transcript.
bullets: Generate share-ready summaries and reports
Build trackers for renewals, obligations, invoices, and follow-up work
Turn important dates into calendar-ready outputs

tabs.label: Private-data controls
tabs.title: Privacy, locality, sanitization, and access controls stay part of the workflow from intake to export.
bullets: Regional options cover supported GCP regions including Saudi, UAE, and the US
Customer files are not used to train models
Sensitive document data can be sanitized or kept out of broader sharing flows
Access can stay limited to the active team with review history available

applications.title: What each acquisition workspace contains.
applications.subhead: The homepage now reflects the sharper product: a mandate-first acquisition operator, not a generic listing dashboard.

cards.title: Mandate Fit
cards.subtitle: See why a property surfaced, where it fits the buy box, and where it fails the investor's constraints.
bullets: Budget, geography, asset type, timeline, financing, and return fit
Clear pursue, pass, request info, visit, negotiate, or quote next step

cards.title: Evidence and Diligence
cards.subtitle: Keep listing data, documents, photos, maps, public-record references, broker claims, and open questions in one deal file.
bullets: Basis labels for verified sources, market signals, modeled outputs, counterparty claims, and uncertainty
Missing documents, contradictions, inspection needs, and broker follow-ups

cards.title: Scenario and Renovation Logic
cards.subtitle: Model price, financing, hold period, rent, operating costs, capex, exit assumptions, and return thresholds.
bullets: Base, upside, and downside scenarios with visible assumptions
Renovation scope ranges, capex bands, confidence levels, and return impact

decisionPack.title: Decision packs your team can actually defend.
bullets: Share-ready summaries for internal review or stakeholder delivery
Key facts and issues linked back to the source
Clear distinction between verified items, review items, and overrides
Export options for PDF, Word, and operational handoff

security.title: Privacy and control, built in.
leftBullets: Customer files are not used to train models
Regional deployment options cover supported GCP regions, including Saudi, UAE, and the US
Workspace access and review history support controlled collaboration
You can choose the model setup that fits your security, cost, and compliance needs

rightCard.title: Privacy and control in practice
bullets: On supported US web workflows, Zohal can process extracted document data without storing the original file on Zohal servers
On iOS, files can stay on device for private workflows
WhatsApp, Google Drive, and OneDrive fit how teams already receive and store files
Outputs stay structured for review, sharing, and follow-up

professional.name: Starter Workspace
bullets: Best for evaluating one live property with discipline
One acquisition workspace for mandate fit, evidence, scenarios, diligence gaps, and next steps
Upload listing material, documents, photos, and broker notes
Evidence labels and review history included

3 workspaces and 2 users for collaboration with a partner, adviser, or family member
Mandate monitoring, candidate ranking, scenario modeling, and diligence tracking

20 acquisition workspaces and 5 users

usageMeter.title: Simple acquisition units
usageMeter.body: Plans are organized around mandates, candidate workspaces, users, renovation estimate workflows, monitoring, and premium coordination. The goal is simple: move from raw opportunity to disciplined decision faster.

insights.title: Where teams use Zohal first.
insights.subhead: Common review jobs where proof matters more than another generic summary.

items.title: Lease reviews with conflicting renewal dates
items.excerpt: Catch date conflicts across the main agreement and later amendments before the memo is sent.

items.title: Arabic/English contract checks before signature
items.excerpt: Compare both language versions and surface translation differences with linked evidence.

items.title: Fast first-pass review for amendment stacks
items.excerpt: Turn a messy bundle into a short, reviewable pack that highlights only what changed.

cta.label: Talk to us about your workflow

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.intro: A few practical questions teams ask before they start.

items.q: How does Zohal use AI on important documents?
items.a: Zohal extracts the important fields from the document, links them back to the source, and runs a second verifier pass so weak or unsupported results stay marked for review.

items.q: Where can our data be processed?
items.a: Supported deployment regions include Saudi Arabia, the UAE, and the US.

items.q: Do you have to store our documents?
items.a: Not always. Supported US web workflows can process extracted data without storing the original file, and iOS private workflows can keep files on device.

items.q: Can we choose which AI models are used?
items.a: Yes. You can choose from multiple models, including open-source options.

items.q: Does it support Arabic and English documents together?
items.a: Yes. Zohal handles Arabic and English documents together, including scans, annexes, stamped pages, and translation or localization review.

items.q: What do we get at the end?
items.a: Share-ready reports, trackers, document search results, and calendar-ready dates.

contactRow.note: Want to see how this fits your workflow? We can tailor a sample output to your use case.

workspaceCockpitPage.subtitle: Decision cockpit for mandate fit, evidence, scenarios, open items, and next action
workspaceCockpitPage.heroBody: Review the evidence, model the deal, expose renovation risk, and decide the next move.
workspaceCockpitPage.heroAnalystThesis: Pursue only if the roof and waterproofing risk is capped, comparable rent evidence supports the base case, and the negotiation anchor stays below the current ask. This is a controlled yes, not an unconditional yes.
workspaceCockpitPage.emptyPosture: Ask Zohal to screen a listing or run a mandate search to populate the acquisition cockpit.
workspaceCockpitPage.decisionDescription: Recommendation, assumptions, and scenario controls live here.
workspaceCockpitPage.scenarioBody: Base case, downside, and renovation/capex assumptions are controlled from the cockpit.
workspaceCockpitPage.capexBody: Condition signals and estimated capex stay modeled until verified by inspection or quote.
workspaceCockpitPage.nextActionReview: Review the latest recommendation or ask Zohal to update assumptions.
workspaceCockpitPage.nextActionSearch: Run search or paste a listing in Search.
workspaceCockpitPage.candidateDescription: Folded into the cockpit until a separate candidate result flow is needed.
workspaceCockpitPage.emptyLog: Notes, channel events, and screening updates will appear here.
workspaceCockpitPage.publishBody: Publish a Living Interface when the workspace is ready to share.
workspaceCockpitPage.mandateMonitoringDescription: The structured brief Zohal uses to search, screen, and notify.
workspaceCockpitPage.editBuyBoxHint: Use short semicolon-separated parts for buy box, target locations, budget, and risk appetite.
workspaceCockpitPage.editBuyBoxPlaceholder: Villa in North Riyadh; Narjis, Hittin, Ar Rabi; SAR 3M-5M; moderate renovation appetite
workspaceCockpitPage.buyBoxSaveError: Unable to save buy box right now.
workspaceCockpitPage.goodMatch: What counts as a good match
workspaceCockpitPage.goodMatchDefault: Mandate fit, evidence quality, capex exposure, and downside risk
workspaceCockpitPage.evidenceBody: Evidence stays organized in Files; the cockpit only shows coverage and decision gaps.
workspaceCockpitPage.overviewClaimBody: {count} source-linked facts are attached to this deal.
workspaceCockpitPage.scenarioSaveError: Unable to save assumptions right now.
workspaceCockpitPage.underwritingKnobsTitle: Run the acquisition math from controlled assumptions.
workspaceCockpitPage.underwritingRunError: Unable to run underwriting right now.
workspaceCockpitPage.underwritingNeedsAssumptionsBody: Purchase price and rent are required before Zohal can calculate returns.
workspaceCockpitPage.noClaims: No source-linked claims are attached yet.

progress.title: Intent to close
progress.nextReadiness: Start buyer readiness before external action.
progress.nextVisit: Schedule a visit or quote inspection.
progress.helper: Follow the active stage, clear blockers, and move the deal forward with approval-gated actions.

info.verified: Facts supported by documents or source records.
info.marketSignal: Signals from listings, comps, or market observations.
info.counterparty: Information provided by a broker, seller, or representative.

workspaceCockpitPage.marketSignalEmpty: No market signal is attached to this opportunity yet.
workspaceCockpitPage.counterpartyEmpty: No broker or counterparty note is attached yet.
workspaceCockpitPage.uncertainEmpty: No explicit uncertainty is flagged right now.
workspaceCockpitPage.modelEmptyTitle: Add deal assumptions to activate the model
workspaceCockpitPage.modelEmptyBody: The cockpit will not invent price, rent, or capex values. Add acquisition price and monthly rent metadata, then the local modeler will become interactive.
workspaceCockpitPage.renovationEmpty: No renovation scope has been captured yet. Keep this as a decision blocker until inspection or quote evidence exists.
workspaceCockpitPage.openItemsEmpty: No open items are flagged for this opportunity right now.
workspaceCockpitPage.compsEmpty: No comparable market context has been attached yet.
workspaceCockpitPage.approvalRequestError: Unable to queue this approval right now.
workspaceCockpitPage.brokerageGateHint: Buyer-side representation must be active before Zohal sends negotiation or offer actions.

buyerReadiness.title: Buyer Readiness
buyerReadiness.emptyBody: Upload proof of funds or a financing letter into the secure financing folder. Zohal records readiness evidence only; it does not underwrite credit.
buyerReadiness.usePrimaryAction: Use the action at the top of this page when this step is next.
buyerReadiness.startError: Unable to start readiness right now.
buyerReadiness.noEvidence: No readiness evidence is attached yet.
buyerReadiness.noSharing: No active document-sharing consent is recorded.
buyerReadiness.noApprovals: No external actions are queued for approval.

workspaceCockpitPage.activeMandateStrip: Active mandate · Riyadh renovation-relevant residential · Investor control surface
workspaceCockpitPage.modelKnobsTitle: Test the assumptions. Watch the decision move.
workspaceCockpitPage.modelSensitivityNote: The deal is most sensitive to renovation overrun and realized rent. Keep the negotiation posture conditional until open items are resolved.
workspaceCockpitPage.renovationModelBody: Changing this budget updates the same scenario assumptions used by Deal financials.
workspaceCockpitPage.renovationNotesPlaceholder: Old apartment, rental-ready refresh, bathrooms uncertain...
workspaceCockpitPage.capexGenerateError: Unable to generate the capex estimate right now.
